author: lOrm
content:
Fix applied to src/tools/bash/mod.rs:

ROOT-CAUSE FIX: moved 'output_retrieved_after_finish.store(true, SeqCst)' from inside an 'if is_finished' gate (pre-fix mod.rs:216-220) to fire unconditionally on every get_output drain (post-fix mod.rs:230-242). This eliminates the documented race: a caller draining BEFORE the wait-thread flips finished=true left the GC flag forever false, so the slot was retained indefinitely.

EVIDENCE:
- cargo test --lib: 1262 passed; 0 failed (baseline 1259 + 3 new fix351_* tests = 1262 exactly).
- cargo clippy --lib --all-targets -- -D warnings: EXIT=0.
- No #[allow], no #[ignore], no clippy.toml additions (verified against the diff).

TESTS ADDED:
1. fix351_drain_before_finish_marks_retrieved (race-exposing): spawn 'sleep 5', drain while is_running=true, assert flag flipped. Pre-fix this test would FAIL because the 'if is_finished' gate skipped the store.
2. fix351_drain_after_finish_marks_retrieved (backward-compat): drain after the short-lived process finishes; flag still set.
3. fix351_never_drained_stays_unretrieved (correctness): spawn, wait for finish but never call get_output; flag remains false so GC must NOT collect.

SCOPE NOTE: the issue body proposed a broader refactor (Mutex<ShellStatus>, background GC thread on a timer, stdout-thread must not set finished). The user explicitly narrowed this task to the GC-flag race only; problems (a)/(b)/(c) in the issue body remain valid but out of scope here.
